brand.name: Nora Michalski
brand.vertical: B2B/professional services — psychology-based performance protocol for high-achievers

summary: Nora Michalski sells The Greenlight Method, a private 1–2 session psychological protocol (explicitly 'not coaching, not therapy') that removes the emotional charge behind procrastination and self-sabotage on high-leverage business actions, for a one-time $2,000 fee. It serves established entrepreneurs, executives, coaches, consultants, and senior professionals who know what to do but keep avoiding it. Leads enter through a free 15-question self-assessment and an application-gated alignment call that Nora closes personally.

campaigns.name: Assessment-to-Call Nurture
campaigns.objective: Move high-intent assessment completers from self-diagnosis to applying for a free alignment call.
campaigns.trigger: Completion of the free 15-question 'Find Out What's Stopping You' assessment.
campaigns.segment: Assessment completers who have not yet applied for a demo/alignment call; branch by the dominant block their answers reveal (visibility, sales avoidance, decision paralysis).
campaigns.rationale: The assessment is Nora's main inbound entry point, and completers who don't book a call fall into a cold black hole — closing that gap is the single fastest revenue lever for a days-to-weeks sales cycle.

emails.purpose: Reframe the assessment result — name that the block is an emotional charge, not a discipline problem — and point to the free alignment call as the way to drop it.
emails.subject: Your assessment results: what's actually stopping you
emails.preview: It's not discipline. It's an emotional charge — and it's droppable.
emails.cta: Apply for your free alignment call
keyPoints: Mirror their answers back: 'You already know exactly what to do — so why does this specific action keep getting avoided?'
Reframe avoidance as a removable emotional charge in the nervous system, not a willpower or productivity failure — this is the category-education the buyer needs
Position The Greenlight Method as 'not coaching, not therapy' and tease the felt, in-the-body shift that happens in 1–2 sessions
Single low-friction CTA to apply for the free, application-gated alignment call with Nora

campaigns.name: Alignment Call → Enrollment
campaigns.objective: Convert applicants and post-call prospects who haven't enrolled by resolving the price and category-skepticism objections.
campaigns.trigger: Demo/alignment call applied for or completed without enrollment.
campaigns.segment: Applicants and post-call prospects who have not enrolled; branch by whether the call happened (warm recap) or was missed (re-book first).
campaigns.rationale: The alignment call is the only close mechanism, and the two objections that stall a $2,000 one-time decision — 'is this worth it?' and 'what exactly am I buying?' — are predictable and answerable in email between touches.

emails.purpose: Handle the #1 objection head-on — define exactly what the protocol is and de-risk the $2,000 decision with the guarantees.
emails.subject: Not coaching, not therapy — here's exactly what you're buying
emails.preview: And why the price reflects the result, not the hours.
emails.cta: Reply to claim your spot (or ask Nora anything)
keyPoints: Define the mechanism plainly: a private protocol that removes the emotional charge on a specific avoided action in 1–2 sessions — distinct from coaching and therapy
Address price directly in the brand's own framing: 'not the cheapest, the most effective' — anchor the $2,000 against months of avoided money-generating action
Stack the de-risk: 7-day money-back guarantee, protocol guarantee (extra sessions free if the charge doesn't drop), bonus audio suite, and 30-day accountability support
Include one short 'results not typical' client proof point and an invitation to reply with the exact action they've been avoiding

campaigns.name: New-Client Welcome & Session Priming
campaigns.objective: Prime newly enrolled clients to notice and trust the somatic shift so the first session lands as a felt 'aha'.
campaigns.segment: New clients between enrollment and their first private session.
campaigns.rationale: The aha is a visceral, in-the-body drop in emotional charge during session one; clients who arrive primed to notice it activate faster and are far more likely to implement while momentum is high.

emails.purpose: Warm welcome, set expectations for the first session, and give one quick prep action that makes the shift easier to feel.
emails.subject: You're in — let's make your first session land
emails.preview: A 2-minute prep so the shift is impossible to miss.
emails.cta: Pick the one action you'll bring to session 1
keyPoints: Welcome them by name and affirm the decision — high-achievers want reassurance they chose the effective path, not the cheap one
Set the expectation that the result is felt in the body, not figured out intellectually, so they know what to watch for
One prep action: name the single high-stakes action they've been avoiding and notice the charge around it before the session
Reassure with the support that comes built in — bonus audio suite, 30-day accountability, and a real human reply address

campaigns.name: Alignment Call Reminders & No-Show Recovery
campaigns.objective: Maximize attendance at the alignment call — the revenue event — and recover the ones that slip.
campaigns.trigger: Alignment call booked; upcoming call; no-show event.
campaigns.segment: Anyone with a scheduled alignment call with Nora.
campaigns.rationale: Nora closes personally on the call, so a missed call is a lost sale; reminders cut no-shows by roughly 35%, recovering revenue immediately on an already application-gated, high-intent audience.

emails.purpose: 24-hour reminder that confirms the time, primes them for the conversation, and removes friction with an easy reschedule link.
emails.subject: Your call with Nora is tomorrow at [time]
emails.preview: Come with the one action you've been avoiding in mind.
keyPoints: Confirm the exact date and time with an add-to-calendar link and the join details
Prime the call: have in mind the specific high-leverage action they keep avoiding — that's what the conversation will work on
Reassure it's a no-pressure alignment call to see if the protocol fits, not a hard pitch — lowers the avoidance that causes no-shows
One-click reschedule link so a conflict becomes a new time, not a silent drop-off

campaigns.name: Cold Lead Win-Back
campaigns.objective: Reactivate assessment takers and lapsed inquiries who went cold without booking or enrolling, and clean the list.
campaigns.trigger: 60–90 days of no clicks/replies since assessment completion or last inquiry.
campaigns.segment: Dormant assessment completers and lapsed prospects who never applied or never enrolled.
campaigns.rationale: Cold assessment takers self-identified a real, costly block and have no re-entry point; win-back recovers 5–15% of inactive leads and protects sender reputation before sunsetting non-responders.

emails.purpose: Pattern-interrupt that reminds them of the cost of the unresolved block and reopens the door with a low-friction next step.
emails.subject: Still avoiding that one thing?
emails.preview: Your assessment said it was costing you. Want to finally drop it?
keyPoints: Acknowledge time has passed and reconnect to what their assessment surfaced — the specific action they've kept avoiding
Name the quiet cost of another quarter of avoidance (lost momentum, eroded self-trust, the energy it drains) without guilt-tripping
Reassure the path is still short and low-risk: a free alignment call, a 1–2 session protocol, and guarantees if it doesn't land
Offer a graceful exit option to protect deliverability — a soft 'should we stop emailing you?' for true non-responders
